For Her — Open
05 — Timeline

FORTY
PLUS YEARS
OF NO.

A working chronology. Not a canon, not a syllabus. Argue with it. That’s the spirit.

  1. 1970s.
    1974BEFORE THE WORD

    Patti Smith fronts a band downtown. Suzi Quatro is already in leather. The Runaways form in California — the wedge starts going in before anyone agrees on the name for what’s coming.

  2. 1976LONDON OPENS

    The Slits form. Siouxsie shows up at the 100 Club. Vivienne Westwood is already cutting up uniforms in a shop on the King’s Road. Punk becomes a word people use.

  3. 1977THE YEAR ZERO

    X-Ray Spex release Oh Bondage Up Yours!. Patti puts out Horses' second pressing. The Slits tour with the Clash. The boys get the singles chart — the women get the manifestos.

  4. 1978-79POST-PUNK BREAKS WIDE

    The Raincoats, Bush Tetras, Au Pairs, Delta 5, Lydia Lunch. The sound stops being three chords and starts being whatever you want — which was, of course, always the point.

  5. 1980s.
    1980sHARDCORE AND AFTER

    Hardcore tries to lock the door again. Women kick it back open: Penelope Houston, Exene, Kira Roessler, Wendy O. Williams, Poly Styrene returning. The next wave keeps writing.

  6. 1990s.
    1991REVOLUTION GIRL STYLE NOW

    Bikini Kill, Bratmobile, Heavens to Betsy, Huggy Bear. Olympia, DC, Leeds. The zine network goes nuclear. “Riot grrrl” is suddenly a beat at every major paper.

  7. 1993-96PRETTY ON THE INSIDE

    Hole, L7, Babes in Toyland, Sleater-Kinney’s debut. The mainstream finally has to deal with what was always there. Some of it gets co-opted; the records remain undefeated.

  8. 2000s.
    2000sQUIET FRONT, LOUD BACK

    The press declares it over a dozen times. Meanwhile: Le Tigre, the Gossip, Erase Errata, Mika Miko. The networks built in 1991 keep paying out.

  9. 2010s.
    2010sRECEIPTS, AT VOLUME

    Savages, Priests, Big Joanie, Downtown Boys, G.L.O.S.S. Documentaries finally arrive. Memoirs land at Knopf. The canon is forced to update itself, slowly, with great complaint.

  10. NOWSTILL UNFINISHED

    This page is also unfinished. That is the point. Hand it to the next person who needs it.

WOMEN/PUNK

A history in noise. Loud, unfinished, ours.
